title: corothamnus balansae
intro: Corothamnus balansae, also known as Tibetan wormwood, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimalarial properties. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, treat malaria, and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Corothamnus balansae is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
